reasoning: RULE A applied: wrong tool called (get_japan_heritage returns 文化庁 日本遺産, NOT UNESCO World Heritage Sites). The tool's own tool_scope_note explicitly warns 'If the user asked about UNESCO World Heritage Sites ... see see_also_wikidata_heritage'. Per RULE B, single root cause (wrong tool) drives the worst dim hit; specificity scores 1 because no UNESCO WHS named assets appear at all. Intent scores 2 because the user's actual query (UNESCO sites) is entirely unaddressed — only browse_advisory redirects for rural life/tea/festivals are returned. Groundedness and factual_accuracy hold at 4 because the Japan Heritage count (104) and browse_advisory routing hints are real and accurate for their own program. Per RULE C, partial authoritative data = 3 for feasibility and expression_quality.
missing_data: Actual UNESCO World Heritage Sites in Japan (e.g. Shirakami-Sanchi, Shiretoko, Ogasawara Islands, Hidden Christian Sites, Jomon Prehistoric Sites — especially lesser-known ones outside Kyoto/Nara/Hiroshima). The see_also_wikidata_heritage pointer in tool_scope_note is the only relevant hint but is buried and not actionable.
improvement_hint: The server should detect UNESCO-intent queries at routing time and auto-pivot to get_traditional_arts or search_hybrid with a UNESCO/世界遺産 filter, or surface see_also_wikidata_heritage as a top-level redirect field rather than burying it in tool_scope_note. Alternatively, add a dedicated get_unesco_whs tool backed by Wikidata P1435 WHS records.
